commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=755514a560faacfb078edc11a73ea69675c63dc8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds refreshable OpenCode Go manifest catalog rows, a sync bundled-catalog fallback in context-token resolution, updated OpenCode Go model metadata, and focused context/static-catalog tests.

PR surface: Source +291, Tests +80. Total +371 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows memory flush uses sync context resolution with async loading disabled and falls back to DEFAULT_CONTEXT_TOKENS, while the linked report gives live evidence of premature compaction for OpenCode Go.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the code has blocking catalog/sanitizer issues and the external contributor still needs to provide real behavior proof that automation cannot supply for them.

target.title: fix(opencode-go): load context windows from bundled catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the `opencode-go` sync context-window fallback so explicit provider lookups can read bundled `refreshable` manifest rows after config and cache misses.
- Expands the bundled `opencode-go` manifest catalog to the 13 requested models with current context windows and output caps for sync callers.
- Keeps the live/runtime OpenCode Go catalog broader than the bundled fallback set so currently served upstream models like `hy3-preview`, `kimi-k2.5`, `mimo-v2-pro`, `mimo-v2-omni`, `qwen3.5-plus`, and `minimax-m2.5` do not disappear from runtime discovery.
- Keeps onboarding from freezing refreshable provider metadata into user config, so future live discovery can still update catalog facts.
- Treats `kimi-k2.7-code` like `kimi-k2.6` for OpenCode Go payload sanitization by stripping unsupported `reasoning_effort` fields.

This matters because issue #92912 is a real session-state regression: memory flush and preflight compaction were falling back to `DEFAULT_CONTEXT_TOKENS` for several `opencode-go` models, so 1M-window models compacted around ~156K instead of their provider-owned limits.

Reviewers should focus on `src/agents/context.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.ts`, and the split between bundled fallback metadata and broader runtime/live OpenCode Go catalog data.

## Linked context

Closes #92912

Related #92913

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes. This PR was created in response to a maintainer request to open a fresh branch, fix the issue on a new branch, and keep the PR body compliant with the Real behavior proof contract.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed:
  `opencode-go` sync context-window lookups were missing bundled metadata for most current models, so memory flush and other sync callers fell back to `200000` instead of provider-owned limits.
- Real environment tested:
  Local source checkout on macOS against the actual `openclaw` repo working tree, including the rebuilt bundled plugin runtime under `dist/extensions`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  `pnpm build`
  `pnpm test src/agents/context.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- Evidence after fix:
  `dist/extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json` now exposes the 13-model bundled fallback set:
  ```json
  {
    "discovery": "refreshable",
    "models": [
      "deepseek-v4-pro",
      "deepseek-v4-flash",
      "glm-5",
      "glm-5.1",
      "kimi-k2.7-code",
      "kimi-k2.6",
      "mimo-v2.5",
      "mimo-v2.5-pro",
      "minimax-m2.7",
      "minimax-m3",
      "qwen3.6-plus",
      "qwen3.7-max",
      "qwen3.7-plus"
    ]
  }
  ```
  Targeted verification after the rebuild passed:
  ```text
  [test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 14.54s
  ```
- Observed result after fix:
  Sync provider lookups now resolve bundled manifest limits for models like `opencode-go/mimo-v2.5-pro`, memory flush callers stop falling back to `200000`, runtime/live discovery still exposes the broader currently served OpenCode Go model set, and onboarding no longer persists refreshable catalog metadata into config.
- What was not tested:
  No live OpenCode Go network call or Crabbox/Testbox proof was rerun in this rebase pass.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test src/agents/context.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/context.test.ts` now asserts sync provider lookups can use bundled `refreshable` manifest rows for `opencode-go/mimo-v2.5-pro`.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts` now asserts memory-flush callers inherit the bundled `opencode-go` manifest limit for `mimo-v2.5-pro`.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts` now covers `includeRefreshableDiscovery: true` for read-only metadata fallbacks.
- `extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts` keeps runtime/live catalog coverage aligned with the broader currently served model set and covers `kimi-k2.7-code` reasoning sanitization.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Issue #92912 showed `opencode-go` models outside the old 2-row bundled manifest subset falling back to the default 200K sync context window.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added and rerun.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is sync model metadata resolution order: the new bundled manifest fallback must stay behind explicit config and runtime cache hits so it does not override provider-specific runtime state.

How is that risk mitigated?

The fallback only runs after config-backed and cache-backed resolution misses, it is limited to explicit provider lookups, the bundled manifest lookup now explicitly opts into `refreshable` rows only for this read-only fallback, and runtime/live catalog coverage remains broader than the bundled static fallback set.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review, then merge once CI is happy with the rebased branch.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Fresh CI on the rebased branch.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

This refresh addresses the real review concern that runtime/live OpenCode Go discovery must remain broader than the bundled 13-model fallback set, and that onboarding must not persist refreshable catalog metadata into user config.
target.author: nianjiuzst

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
